mechanism: Binary classification of observed events as intentional or unintentional based on contextual information, followed by behavioral persistence measurement in response to motivation signals. Higher motivation state indicated by increased effort, rate, and duration of voluntary action selection in reinforcement-based tasks compared to control conditions. Motivation inferred from choice prevalence and response elasticity under variable reward schedules.

Multi-player game with simultaneous decisions: two disputants choose cooperation or competition; third party chooses intervention to add monetary incentives that alter payoff structure. Intervention transforms competitive game into cooperative game by inverting net payoffs for defection relative to cooperation. Disputant behavior responds monotonically to third-party intervention probability.

Multivariate linear regression and mediation analysis linking postural features (binary expansion vs. contraction) to attraction ratings and selection outcomes. Perceived dominance serves as mediator between posture and attraction. Analysis of variance across two field contexts (speed-dating, online dating) and one laboratory mediation study using Monte Carlo confidence intervals.

Hierarchical linear modeling with cross-level interaction: income (level 1, individual) nested within state-level inequality (level 2, Gini coefficient). Interaction term tests whether income-generosity slope varies by inequality. Experimental manipulation replicates by portraying high or low inequality, predicting individual giving behavior via generosity measure. Mediation tested at domain of applicability.

Binary outcome measurement (pain rating on 0-10 scale; treatment recommendation accurate/inaccurate) regressed on target race, false-belief endorsement, and their interaction. Mediation tests whether racial bias in pain perception mediates the effect of false beliefs on treatment accuracy. Within-subjects repeated measures across two medical case vignettes.

Between-subjects and within-subjects designs manipulating information disclosure (reveal vs. withhold vs. inadvertent nondisclosure) and measuring outcomes: categorical choice (date/hire preference), continuous trust judgment, and behavioral allocation in trust game (dollar transfer). Mediation analysis tests trustworthiness as mechanism underlying choice bias against withholders.
